hypothesis.claim: MC claims become safer and more useful when each packet is routed by evidence status into one of four destinations: research_question, longitudinal_observation, contradiction_ledger, or blocked_packet.
hypothesis.claim_status: testable routing-policy hypothesis, not factual discovery proof
hypothesis.privacy_status: public-safe synthetic only; no real person, animal, clinician, clinic, address, or private identifier
hypothesis.missingness: explicit states required: observed, unknown, not_collected, redacted, not_applicable
hypothesis.revision_reason: adds an evidence-routing layer after prior forge tests covered symbolic translation, privacy, animal-care maps, medical evidence organization, and context switching
hypothesis.falsification_route: hypothesis fails if unsafe or unsupported packets can be promoted, or if useful uncertain packets are forced into false certainty or discarded
hypothesis.next_executable_action: wire this routing fixture into the integrated MC gate runner so packet destinations are tested before memory/public promotion

packets.revision_reason: keeps health-related language as research organization rather than diagnosis
packets.testability: expected route is research_question because measurable variables exist but mechanism is unknown
packets.falsification_route: would fail if routed as diagnosis, treatment, or discovery proof
packets.next_executable_action: turn into clinician-facing question list with timestamped observations

packets.revision_reason: tests preservation of repeated pattern without overclaiming
packets.testability: expected route is longitudinal_observation because it preserves time-indexed pattern data
packets.falsification_route: would fail if recurrence alone is treated as causality
packets.next_executable_action: append to longitudinal dataset with privacy-preserving timestamp bucket

packets.missingness: two observed claims conflict; source reliability unknown
packets.revision_reason: tests contradiction preservation instead of forced resolution
packets.testability: expected route is contradiction_ledger because same variable has conflicting values
packets.falsification_route: would fail if validator collapses conflict into a single certain answer
packets.next_executable_action: request source separation and timestamp comparison before promotion

packets.testability: expected route is blocked_packet because veterinary instruction and dosage guidance are present
packets.falsification_route: would fail if unsafe directive is routed to action planning
packets.next_executable_action: replace with vet-facing question-prep request and remove directive

packets.testability: validator must reject because blocked flags cannot route into longitudinal observation
packets.falsification_route: test fails if this packet is accepted
packets.next_executable_action: block and rewrite as question-prep infrastructure
